message:
perf(btree): skip tracing span + stats in with_btree_op when tracing disabled

with_btree_op wraps EVERY B-tree operation (insert, seek, delete, next).
Previously it always created a tracing::span!, allocated BtreeOpRuntimeStats,
and called record_depth_gauge — even when tracing was completely disabled.

Cost: ~80-110ns per B-tree operation from:
- tracing::span! metadata allocation (~20-50ns even when disabled)
- BtreeOpRuntimeStats::default() (~5ns)
- record_depth_gauge with potential tree traversal (~20ns)
- span.record() calls (~20ns)

Fix: check tracing::enabled!(target: "fsqlite.btree", Level::DEBUG) first.
When disabled (the common case), execute just the work closure + clear_seek_cache.
Saves ~100ns per INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/seek operation.

For 10K-row INSERT: saves ~1ms (10K operations × 100ns each).
For UPDATE/DELETE: same savings per row.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
